messages.content: You output JSON for task plans.

You are a senior project planner producing structured task plans.

Follow this JSON schema:
TaskPlan { originalPrompt: string; subtasks: Subtask[]; totalSubtasks: number; decompositionStrategy?: string }
Subtask { id: string (kebab-case), title: string, description: string (>=10 chars), status: one of pending|in_progress|completed|failed, dependencies: string[], estimatedComplexity: low|medium|high, successCriteria: string }
Constraints: 2-10 subtasks, actionable descriptions, include logical dependencies, avoid circular references, IDs unique.
Original prompt: Framework: Express, Styling: Tailwind CSS

Original request: create hello world app, where i can modify the text "hello world" colors, through a frontend ui
Clarifications:
- framework: Express
- styling: Tailwind CSS
Previous validation issues:
None
Respond ONLY with JSON matching the schema.

response:
{
  "originalPrompt": "Framework: Express, Styling: Tailwind CSS\nOriginal request: create hello world app, where i can modify the text \"hello world\" colors, through a frontend ui\nClarifications:\n- framework: Express\n- styling: Tailwind CSS",
  "subtasks": [
    {
      "id": "project-initialization",
      "title": "Initialize project repository and npm",
      "description": "Create project folder, initialize git repo and package.json, add basic folder structure (server/, public/, src/), and define start/dev scripts.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "Repository initialized with package.json, git committed, folders created, and npm scripts for start and dev present."
    },
    {
      "id": "install-dependencies",
      "title": "Install runtime and build dependencies",
      "description": "Install Express and development tools (tailwindcss, postcss, autoprefixer), plus dev utilities (nodemon, concurrently) via npm or yarn.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "project-initialization"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "Dependencies listed in package.json and node_modules installed; dev utilities available in npm scripts."
    },
    {
      "id": "configure-tailwind",
      "title": "Set up Tailwind build pipeline",
      "description": "Create tailwind.config.js and postcss.config.js, add Tailwind directives to source CSS, configure content paths, and add build/watch scripts to package.json.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "install-dependencies"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Tailwind config files exist, build script produces a CSS file containing Tailwind utilities, and watch mode works for development."
    },
    {
      "id": "create-express-server",
      "title": "Implement Express server to serve static frontend",
      "description": "Create app entry (e.g., app.js), configure Express to serve built static files from public/, set up root route serving index.html, and add dev start command.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "install-dependencies"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Server starts without errors and serves index.html and static assets at http://localhost:{port}."
    },
    {
      "id": "build-frontend-hello-world",
      "title": "Create frontend Hello World page with Tailwind",
      "description": "Add index.html and frontend JS that renders the \"Hello World\" text styled with Tailwind classes and includes the compiled Tailwind CSS.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "configure-tailwind",
        "create-express-server"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Visiting the server shows the styled \"Hello World\" text using Tailwind and the page loads CSS and JS correctly."
    },
    {
      "id": "implement-color-picker-ui",
      "title": "Add frontend UI to choose text color",
      "description": "Implement a color picker control (input[type=color] or swatches) and UI to select text color; JS should apply the chosen color to the Hello World element in real time.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "build-frontend-hello-world"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Selecting a color immediately updates the Hello World text color in the page and the control is usable on desktop and mobile."
    },
    {
      "id": "implement-live-update-and-endpoints",
      "title": "Optional: persist and expose color via API",
      "description": "Add simple Express endpoints to GET/POST the chosen color (in-memory or file), and wire the frontend to save/load the color via fetch so selection persists across reloads.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "implement-color-picker-ui",
        "create-express-server"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Frontend can save and load the selected color through the API and the color persists across page reloads."
    },
    {
      "id": "testing-and-documentation",
      "title": "Test app and create README",
      "description": "Perform manual tests (start dev, build, change colors, persist check), fix issues, and add README with setup, run, and usage instructions plus troubleshooting notes.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "implement-live-update-and-endpoints",
        "build-frontend-hello-world"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "README explains setup and usage; manual tests pass and any found bugs are documented or fixed."
    }
  ],
  "totalSubtasks": 9,
  "decompositionStrategy": "Break the request into environment setup, dependency installation, styling pipeline configuration, server creation, frontend implementation, interactivity (color picker), optional persistence API, and final testing/documentation. Tasks are ordered to ensure prerequisites (build tools and server) exist before frontend development and interactivity. Each subtask is actionable and depends only on earlier tasks to avoid circular dependencies."
}
